hero_subtitle: Novaural develops patent-protected verification systems that transform how hearing professionals optimise hearing aid fittings. We are now seeking clinical partners to validate our platform in real-world settings.
about_subtitle: Novaural is a UK-registered medical deep-tech company dedicated to closing the gap between prescribed hearing and real-world outcomes.
about_text_1: Hearing aids are sophisticated devices, yet fitting them remains heavily dependent on subjective assessments and trial-and-error approaches. At Novaural, we believe every patient deserves a fitting that is objectively verified and quantitatively optimised.
about_text_2: Our approach combines phonemic discrimination science, signal processing engineering, and clinical protocol design into intelligent tools that empower hearing professionals to achieve better outcomes — faster.
about_mission_text: To make evidence-based hearing aid verification accessible, efficient, and standard practice worldwide.
about_approach_text: Deep-tech rooted in peer-reviewed audiology science — not assumptions, not anecdotes.
about_uk_text: Registered and operating from the United Kingdom, serving clinics globally.
about_card_text_1: Today, hearing aid fittings rely on patients describing their subjective experience. The result? Multiple follow-up visits, inconsistent outcomes, and frustrated patients who sometimes abandon their devices entirely.
about_card_text_2: We are developing technology that gives clinicians objective, quantitative data about how a patient actually perceives speech — transforming guesswork into evidence.
about_stat_filed: UKIPO Filed · EU & US in progress
challenge_title:
Hearing Aid Fitting Is Still
Trial and Error
challenge_subtitle: Despite advances in hearing aid technology, the fitting process itself has barely changed. Three key problems persist across the industry.
challenge_card1_text: Patients return repeatedly because fittings are adjusted based on vague feedback — "it sounds too tinny" or "I can't hear in restaurants". Each visit costs time and money with no guarantee of improvement.
challenge_card2_text: Clinicians lack tools that quantify how well a patient actually discriminates speech sounds after fitting. Without data, adjustments are educated guesses at best.
challenge_card3_text: Industry data shows significant hearing aid abandonment. Patients who don't experience clear improvement quickly are likely to stop using their devices altogether.
product_subtitle: A working software platform that gives clinicians the data they need to optimise fittings — quantitatively. Currently operational and seeking clinical validation partners.
product_feat1_text: Scientifically validated minimal pairs test how patients perceive individual speech contrasts (e.g., /s/ vs /θ/, /p/ vs /b/) — revealing precisely where the fitting falls short.
product_feat2_text: Our patented algorithm translates phonemic errors into specific, per-frequency gain adjustments — replacing guesswork with evidence-based recommendations.
product_feat3_text: Tests at 50, 65, and 80 dB SPL verify audibility, intelligibility, and comfort in a single structured session covering real-world listening conditions.
product_feat4_text: Modular language-pack architecture with support for English (UK) and Spanish, with additional languages ready to be added.
product_patent: Patent Pending — Filed at UKIPO · 47 Claims · EU & US filing in progress
hiw_subtitle: A structured, evidence-based workflow that integrates seamlessly into existing clinical practice.
hiw_step1_text: Enter audiogram, current gains, and clinical context. The system configures the optimal test protocol automatically.
hiw_step2_text: Guided phoneme discrimination testing at multiple levels, with speech-in-noise and directionality verification.
hiw_step3_text: The algorithm maps phonemic errors to frequency bands and calculates precise gain adjustments for each channel.
hiw_step4_text: Apply recommendations in your fitting software, then re-test to confirm measurable improvement in discrimination.
hiw_chart_subtitle: Only frequency bands where the patient shows a measured speech discrimination deficit are modified. Healthy bands remain untouched.
stage_subtitle: Our core technology is built, our intellectual property is protected, and we are actively seeking partners to validate the platform in clinical settings.
stage_rd_text: Algorithm design based on peer-reviewed audiology literature. Multi-level phonemic discrimination protocol developed and tested.
stage_sw_text: Working desktop application with full test workflow, quantitative adjustment engine, patient management, and bilingual support (EN/ES).
stage_ip_text: Patent application filed with UKIPO — 47 claims covering the core algorithm, testing methodology, and adjustment engine.
stage_cv_text: Seeking NHS trusts, private clinics, and research institutions to participate in clinical validation pilots. This is our current focus.
stage_reg_text: Regulatory compliance, commercial launch, and wider deployment across audiology services.
partners_subtitle: Novaural is seeking clinical validation partners across the UK hearing care ecosystem. If your organisation is interested in improving fitting outcomes through evidence-based technology, we'd love to talk.
partners_nhs_text: Pilot programmes to validate impact on waiting lists, follow-up rates, and patient outcomes.
partners_private_text: Early adoption opportunities with direct technical support and co-development of clinical workflows.
partners_research_text: Collaborative research into phonemic discrimination methods and fitting verification science.
benefits_subtitle: ReFittingLab creates value across the entire hearing care ecosystem — from NHS trusts to private practices to patients.
benefits_nhs_1: Reduce follow-up visits — Objective verification in the first session means fewer return appointments clogging waiting lists.
benefits_nhs_2: Shorten waiting lists — Faster, more efficient fitting sessions free up clinic capacity for new patients.
benefits_nhs_3: Standardise quality — Quantitative protocols reduce variability between clinicians and clinics.
benefits_nhs_4: Evidence for commissioners — Measurable outcomes data supports service quality reporting and funding justification.
benefits_private_1: Differentiating technology — Offer patients verifiable proof of fitting quality that competitors cannot match.
benefits_private_2: Reduce staff costs — Shorter sessions and fewer follow-ups mean lower cost per patient.
benefits_private_3: Higher conversion rates — Patients who experience measurable improvement in-session are more likely to commit.
benefits_private_4: Lower return rates — Getting the fitting right the first time reduces costly device returns and exchanges.

beyond_subtitle: Novaural's patent-protected technology isn't limited to hearing aids. It can be applied to any consumer audio device with adjustable frequency output — from AirPods and wireless earbuds to headphones, AR/VR headsets, and audio-enabled eyewear.
beyond_problem_subtitle: Millions of people struggle to understand speech through their earbuds — not because they need hearing aids, but because their ears have subtle frequency gaps that a simple volume increase cannot fix.
beyond_card1_text: When speech sounds unclear, the natural reaction is to turn up the volume. But this amplifies all frequencies equally — including those you hear perfectly well, exposing healthy frequencies to potentially damaging sound pressure levels over time.
beyond_card2_text: Even when you "can hear" through your earbuds, your brain is constantly working harder to fill in the gaps at frequencies where your perception is weaker. This unconscious effort causes listening fatigue — making calls, podcasts, and music draining over extended use.
beyond_card3_text: Age-related hearing changes begin as early as your 30s, typically affecting high frequencies first. Most people don't notice — they unknowingly compensate with volume, gradually damaging the frequencies that still work well.
beyond_solution_subtitle: Instead of amplifying everything, Novaural identifies exactly which speech frequencies you struggle with — and adjusts only those, leaving healthy frequencies untouched.
beyond_chart_label: Per-band adjustment example — only deficit bands modified
beyond_text_1: Our technology evaluates your speech sound discrimination — the ability to tell apart similar-sounding speech sounds like "thin" vs "sin" or "fan" vs "van" — at specific frequencies using a quick, scientifically validated test.
beyond_text_2: Based on your results, the system generates precise, per-frequency adjustments that can be applied to the equaliser of any compatible device. Only the frequencies where you show a measured deficit are modified — typically by just 1–3 dB.
beyond_text_3: The result? Speech becomes clearer without any overall volume increase. Your brain no longer needs to strain to decode what it's hearing, and your healthy frequencies remain protected at their natural levels.
beyond_benefits_subtitle: A subtle, intelligent adjustment to how your device delivers audio can have a transformative impact on your daily listening experience.
beyond_safe_1: No need to increase overall volume to understand speech
beyond_safe_2: Healthy frequencies remain at their natural, safe levels
beyond_safe_3: Prevents gradual noise-induced damage from daily over-amplification
beyond_safe_4: Adjustments are bounded by acoustic safety limits built into the algorithm
beyond_safe_5: Especially critical for users who listen for extended periods (commuters, remote workers, students)
beyond_fatigue_1: When speech frequencies are at the correct level, your brain processes audio with less effort
beyond_fatigue_2: Reduces the unconscious strain of "filling in the gaps" for weak frequencies
beyond_fatigue_3: Calls, meetings, and podcasts feel less tiring over long sessions
beyond_fatigue_4: Improved comfort during all-day earbud use
beyond_fatigue_5: Benefit compounds over weeks and months of daily use
beyond_obj_text: Unlike generic "sound profiles" or EQ presets based on preference, Novaural's adjustments are derived from a psychophysically validated speech discrimination test — measuring actual perception, not opinion. Two listeners with identical hearing tests may need different adjustments based on how their brain processes speech, and our technology captures this.
beyond_lang_text: The system adapts to the phonemic structure of any language via interchangeable configuration files. Whether you listen predominantly in English, Spanish, Mandarin, or any other language, the evaluation and adjustments are calibrated to the specific speech sounds that matter in your language.
beyond_device_text: Any consumer audio device with adjustable frequency bands — AirPods, Galaxy Buds, Sony WF-1000XM, over-ear headphones, bone conduction devices, AR/VR headsets — can benefit. The technology adapts to whatever equaliser architecture your device uses, from 5-band to 20+ band systems.
beyond_science_title: Backed by Science, Protected by Patent
beyond_science_text: Built on peer-reviewed audiology research and the Jakobson–Fant–Halle distinctive feature system. The methodology — from speech sound evaluation to frequency-specific output modification — is covered by our 47-claim patent application filed with UKIPO.
beyond_who_subtitle: You don't need a hearing aid to benefit from smarter audio. Novaural's technology helps anyone whose perception across the speech frequency spectrum isn't perfectly uniform — which includes most adults over 30.
beyond_who_adults_text: Age-related high-frequency changes begin in your 30s. Most people don't notice until it becomes significant — but the subtle gaps already affect clarity through earbuds.
beyond_who_remote_text: Hours of video calls through headsets leads to fatigue. When speech frequencies are properly balanced, meetings feel less draining and comprehension improves in noisy environments.
beyond_who_students_text: Listening to lectures, language courses, or audiobooks through earbuds becomes clearer and less tiring, especially when studying in a second language with unfamiliar phonemes.
beyond_who_music_text: Beyond speech, frequency-specific calibration can reveal details in music you've been missing — vocal harmonics, hi-hat sibilance, subtle instrument textures — without over-amplifying the mix.
beyond_who_gamers_text: Spatial audio and in-game dialogue clarity in AR/VR headsets and gaming headphones benefits from a frequency profile matched to your actual perception, not a generic curve.
beyond_who_travellers_text: When using noise-cancelling earbuds in planes, trains, or busy environments, personalised frequency adjustment makes speech-based content (calls, podcasts, audiobooks) clearer without needing unsafe volume levels.
beyond_devices_subtitle: Our technology is device-agnostic. If your device has adjustable frequency bands — whether via a built-in equaliser, firmware API, or companion app — it's compatible.
beyond_dev_earbuds_text: AirPods, Galaxy Buds, Sony, Bose, and any TWS with EQ control
beyond_dev_headphones_text: Over-ear, on-ear, open-back, closed-back — wired or wireless
beyond_dev_arvr_text: Meta Quest, Apple Vision Pro, or any headset with audio output
beyond_dev_eyewear_text: Smart glasses, bone conduction devices, and any host device with adjustable audio
beyond_cta_text: We're looking for consumer audio manufacturers, platform developers, and technology partners to bring frequency-specific speech intelligence to everyday listening devices. If you're building the next generation of personal audio, let's talk.
hiw_how_does_it_sound: "How does it sound?" — Clinicians ask subjective questions with no quantifiable answers
hiw_trial_and_error_adjust: Trial and error — Adjust gains, send patient home, wait for next appointment
hiw_35_return_visits_each: 3–5 return visits — Each one essentially repeating the same subjective loop
hiw_no_phonemelevel_data_if: No phoneme-level data — If the patient says "I can't understand speech," the clinician doesn't know which frequencies are causing the issue
hiw_patient_frustration_30_of: Patient frustration — 30% of hearing aid users abandon their devices within 12 months
hiw_objective_phoneme_discrimination_sco: Objective phoneme discrimination scores — Per-contrast, per-frequency, quantified across 3 intensity levels
hiw_targeted_adjustments_the_multifactor: Targeted adjustments — The multi-factor algorithm tells you exactly which bands need adjustment, calibrated to the patient's audiometric profile
hiw_5_safety_validations_ucl: 5 safety validations — UCL proximity, loudness summation, dynamic range, MPO headroom, and compression ratio are checked before any recommendation
hiw_fewer_visits_more_gets: Fewer visits — More gets resolved in the first session because you have data, not guesses
hiw_clinical_documentation_every_decisio: Clinical documentation — Every decision is evidence-based, auditable, and IEC 62304 traceable
hiw_the_verification_gap_in: The verification gap in current practice
hiw_where_refittinglab_fits_in: Where ReFittingLab Fits in Your Workflow
hiw_fitting_session_ends_after: Fitting session ends after REM verification — no measurement of actual word understanding
hiw_patient_goes_home_with: Patient goes home with "come back in a few days and tell me how it sounds"
hiw_if_unhappy_clinician_adjusts: If unhappy, clinician adjusts gains based on vague subjective feedback with no data on which sounds are problematic
hiw_35_return_visits_each_2: 3–5 return visits, each repeating the same subjective loop — costly for the clinic, frustrating for the patient
hiw_no_documentation_of_finetuning: No documentation of fine-tuning rationale — if audited, no evidence trail of why adjustments were made
hiw_after_rem_run_a: After REM, run a 20-minute verification session — objective measurement of the patient's actual word discrimination
hiw_algorithm_identifies_exactly_which: Algorithm identifies exactly which frequency bands need adjustment and by how much — validated against 5 safety checks
hiw_apply_the_recommendations_in: Apply the recommendations in your fitting software, re-test, and confirm measurable improvement — all in the same session
hiw_patient_leaves_with_a: Patient leaves with a data-verified fitting — fewer return visits, higher satisfaction, lower abandonment
hiw_full_audit_trail_every: Full audit trail: every test, every recommendation, every re-test is documented and IEC 62304 traceable
hiw_standard_audiometric_evaluation_airb: Standard audiometric evaluation: air/bone conduction, UCL, speech recognition threshold. This is your baseline.
hiw_apply_your_formula_of: Apply your formula of choice — NAL-NL2, DSLv5.0, or a manufacturer-based formula (Phonak APD, Oticon VAC+, etc.) — to generate initial gain targets.
hiw_program_the_hearing_aids: Program the hearing aids in Phonak Target, Oticon Genie 2, ReSound Smart Fit, Signia Connexx, Widex Compass GPS, or any other fitting software. Configure coupling, feedback management, features.
hiw_verify_that_the_acoustic: Verify that the acoustic output at the eardrum matches prescriptive targets. This confirms the physical delivery of sound — but not the perceptual outcome.
hiw_apply_refittinglabs_recommendations_: Apply ReFittingLab's recommendations in your fitting software. Adjust G65 for conversational speech, G50 for soft sounds, G80 for loud environments — each adjustment mapped to a specific phoneme discrimination deficit and validated against the patient's dynamic range.
hiw_retest_with_refittinglab_to: Re-test with ReFittingLab to confirm improvement. Document results with statistical significance verification (Thornton-Raffin critical difference). Discharge with confidence — or schedule follow-up only if objective data warrants it.
hiw_what_happens_during_a: What Happens During a ReFittingLab Session
hiw_enter_the_audiogram_airbone: Enter the audiogram (air/bone conduction, UCL), current gain tables (G50/G65/G80/CR/MPO from your fitting software), and clinical context: experience level, tinnitus, hyperacusis, coupling type, bilateral status.
hiw_based_on_the_audiometric: Based on the audiometric profile, the system selects which phoneme contrasts to prioritise. Steep high-frequency loss? Prioritise fricatives (/s/ vs /θ/). Flat moderate loss? Include stops and nasals. Severe HF loss? Flag for frequency lowering verification.
hiw_calibrated_minimal_pair_stimuli: Calibrated minimal pair stimuli — each pair differing in exactly one phonemic segment — are presented via a forced-choice paradigm. The patient selects what they hear: each error maps to a specific frequency band through the distinctive feature → frequency correspondence table.
hiw_extend_to_50_db: Extend to 50 dB (audibility of soft sounds) and 80 dB (comfort and tolerance). This maps to G50, G65, and G80 adjustments across the full dynamic range — not just conversational level.
hiw_sentences_are_presented_with: Sentences are presented with calibrated noise (ICRA/ISTS) at multiple signal-to-noise ratios (+5, 0, −5 dB). Calculates the patient's SNR loss — how many extra dB of signal they need compared to a normal listener.
hiw_the_algorithm_maps_each: The algorithm maps each phoneme error to its acoustic correlate (distinctive feature → frequency band → NFM band matching), then computes the gain delta using a multi-factor formula that considers hearing threshold, dynamic range, session history, and effective audibility. Every recommendation passes through 5 independent safety checks before presentation.
hiw_take_the_recommendations_and: Take the recommendations and apply them directly in Phonak Target, Oticon Genie 2, ReSound Smart Fit, or whichever fitting software you use. ReFittingLab tells you what to change, why, and how much is safe — you stay in control.
hiw_readminister_the_tests_that: Re-administer the tests that showed deficits. If the adjustment was correct, discrimination scores improve measurably. Statistical significance is verified using Thornton-Raffin critical difference tables, with full longitudinal tracking across sessions.
hiw_from_phoneme_error_to: From Phoneme Error to Safe Gain Adjustment
hiw_based_on_ching_dillon: Based on Ching, Dillon et al. (2001, NAL-NL2): as hearing loss increases, the ability to extract speech information diminishes even when sound is audible. The algorithm scales adjustments per-band based on the patient's threshold — not a naive flat boost. At 66 dB HL, contribution drops ~50%.
hiw_band_importance_follows_ansi: Band importance follows ANSI S3.5-1997 Speech Intelligibility Index weights. The 2–4 kHz region contributes ~54% of speech intelligibility — adjustments in these bands are computed and prioritised proportionally.
hiw_firstsession_adjustments_are_conserv: First-session adjustments are conservatively bounded. Subsequent sessions can build on previous data, with the algorithm tracking longitudinal improvement per contrast per band — enabling precise, progressive optimisation.
hiw_the_gain_delta_is: The gain delta is bounded by each band's available dynamic range (UCL − threshold). Patients with narrow dynamic range (< 30 dB) receive proportionally smaller adjustments to preserve comfort headroom.
hiw_the_core_technology_that: The core technology that makes ReFittingLab universally compatible: NFM automatically maps each speech-relevant frequency to the closest adjustable band of any hearing device — whether it has 4 bands (basic aid), 10 bands (mid-range), or 20 bands (premium clinical device). The system consolidates multiple speech frequency regions that fall within the resolution of a single band, weighting each by the magnitude of the discrimination deficit and spectral proximity. One evaluation → any device → optimal result regardless of band count.
hiw_discrimination_data_is_deviceindepen: Discrimination data is device-independent — it characterises the patient's perceptual profile, not the hearing device. When the patient changes devices, only steps (b)–(d) are re-executed with the new band configuration. No re-evaluation needed.
hiw_estimates_the_postadjustment_output: Estimates the post-adjustment output level and verifies it stays at least 7 dB below the patient's Uncomfortable Loudness Level (ASHA guideline: 5–10 dB margin). If exceeded, the system caps the recommendation and flags the band.
hiw_when_3_or_more: When 3 or more high-frequency bands receive simultaneous gain increases, perceived loudness exceeds their arithmetic sum (Keidser & Moore). The system applies a 1.5× summation factor and limits total perceived HF increase to 8 dB per session.
hiw_for_patients_with_narrow: For patients with narrow dynamic range (< 30 dB between threshold and UCL), adjustments exceeding 3 dB are flagged with specific warnings. The recommendation is bounded to preserve comfortable headroom.
hiw_crossreferences_the_proposed_adjustm: Cross-references the proposed adjustment against the hearing device's Maximum Power Output to ensure the recommendation doesn't push the output level into saturation. Requires the actual gain table from fitting software.
hiw_for_frequency_bands_involved: For frequency bands involved in temporal contrasts (voiced/voiceless stop consonants: /p/ vs /b/, /t/ vs /d/, /k/ vs /g/), the system detects compression ratios exceeding 3:1 — which degrade Voice Onset Time discrimination (up to 19.4% intelligibility loss).
hiw_works_with_your_existing: Works With Your Existing Fitting Software
hiw_place_the_sound_level: Place the sound level meter at the patient position (typically 1 metre from the speaker)
hiw_the_software_plays_calibrated: The software plays calibrated reference tones through the loudspeaker
hiw_read_the_spl_from: Read the SPL from the meter and enter it — the software auto-adjusts output to match the target levels (50, 65, 80 dB SPL)
hiw_calibration_is_stored_only: Calibration is stored — only needs re-doing if you change the room, speaker, or position
hiw_reduce_followup_visits_objective: Reduce follow-up visits — Objective verification in the first session means fewer return appointments clogging waiting lists.
hiw_shorten_waiting_lists_faster: Shorten waiting lists — Faster, more efficient fitting sessions free up clinic capacity for new patients.
hiw_standardise_quality_quantitative_pro: Standardise quality — Quantitative protocols reduce variability between clinicians and clinics across trusts.
hiw_evidence_for_commissioners_measurabl: Evidence for commissioners — Measurable outcomes data supports service quality reporting and funding justification (CQC audit-ready).
hiw_differentiating_technology_offer_pat: Differentiating technology — Offer patients verifiable proof of fitting quality that competitors cannot match.
hiw_reduce_staff_costs_shorter: Reduce staff costs — Shorter sessions and fewer follow-ups mean lower cost per patient and higher throughput.
hiw_higher_conversion_rates_patients: Higher conversion rates — Patients who experience measurable improvement in-session are more likely to commit to purchase.
hiw_lower_return_rates_getting: Lower return rates — Getting the fitting right the first time reduces costly device returns and exchanges.
hiw_instead_of_how_does: Instead of "How does it sound?", you get "/s/ vs /θ/ discrimination: 68% → needs +1.19 dB at 4 kHz (bounded by EA factor 0.85, DR factor 0.70)". Every adjustment has a reason. Every reason has data.
hiw_refittinglab_doesnt_suggest_boost: ReFittingLab doesn't suggest "boost highs." It identifies which frequency band is causing which phoneme confusion through distinctive feature analysis, and calculates the minimum effective adjustment — typically 0.5–3 dB, bounded by 5 safety layers.
hiw_adjust_retest_confirm_in: Adjust → Re-test → Confirm. In the same session. Statistical significance is verified using Thornton-Raffin critical difference tables. The system tracks longitudinal improvement per contrast per band across sessions.
hiw_every_test_result_every: Every test result, every recommendation, every safety check, every re-test is recorded in an IEC 62304 traceable audit trail. Useful for clinical governance, audits (NHS/CQC), and longitudinal patient outcomes tracking.
hiw_datadriven_adjustments_resolve_more: Data-driven adjustments resolve more issues in the first fitting session. The traditional 3–5 visit cycle compresses — better outcomes, happier patients, more efficient clinics.
hiw_the_system_uses_interchangeable: The system uses interchangeable language packs with language-specific phoneme inventories, minimal pairs curated through rigorous phonological criteria, and fully localised clinical terminology. Currently calibrated for English, with the architecture designed to support any additional language — simply add a validated language pack and the entire system adapts automatically.
hiw_ready_to_see_it: Ready to See It in Action?
priv_summary_novaural_ltd_respects: Summary: Novaural Ltd respects your privacy. We collect minimal data, only through our partnership enquiry form. We do not use analytics, advertising trackers, or third-party cookies. Your data is never sold or shared for marketing purposes.
priv_novaural_ltd_is_a: Novaural Ltd is a company registered in England & Wales (Company No. 17120454), with its registered address at 71-75 Shelton Street, Covent Garden, London, WC2H 9JQ, United Kingdom.
priv_for_data_protection_purposes: For data protection purposes, Novaural Ltd is the data controller. If you have any questions about how we handle your data, please contact us at enquiries@novaural.com.
priv_we_only_collect_personal: We only collect personal data that you voluntarily provide through our Partnership Enquiry Form on the Partner With Us page. This may include:
priv_a_freetext_message_describing: A free-text message describing your enquiry
priv_we_do_not_collect: We do not collect data through any other means on this website.
priv_the_data_you_submit: The data you submit through the partnership form is used solely to:
priv_evaluate_potential_partnership_or: Evaluate potential partnership or collaboration opportunities
priv_maintain_a_record_of: Maintain a record of our professional communications
priv_we_will_never_use: We will never use your data for marketing newsletters, advertising, or any purpose other than responding to and managing your enquiry.
priv_under_the_uk_general: Under the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR) and the Data Protection Act 2018, we process your personal data on the basis of:
priv_legitimate_interest_article_61f: Legitimate interest (Article 6(1)(f)) — to respond to professional enquiries about partnership opportunities.
priv_consent_by_voluntarily_submitting: Consent — by voluntarily submitting the form, you consent to us processing the data you provide for the stated purpose.
priv_your_data_is_not: Your data is not sold, rented, or shared with third parties for their own purposes. However, we use the following service provider to process form submissions:
priv_formspree_a_secure_form: Formspree — a secure form processing service that receives your form data and forwards it to us via email. Formspree processes data in accordance with their own privacy policy. Data transmitted to Formspree is encrypted in transit (HTTPS/TLS).
priv_we_do_not_use: We do not use any other third-party services that process your personal data (no analytics platforms, no advertising networks, no social media tracking pixels).
priv_this_website_does_not: This website does not use cookies for tracking, analytics, or advertising.
priv_we_may_store_a: We may store a language preference in your browser's localStorage (a browser feature similar to cookies) if multi-language support is activated in the future. This is a strictly functional preference and contains no personal data. It is stored only on your device and is never transmitted to our servers.
priv_you_can_clear_localstorage: You can clear localStorage at any time through your browser settings.
priv_we_retain_partnership_enquiry: We retain partnership enquiry data for as long as necessary to evaluate and respond to the enquiry, and for a reasonable period thereafter in case of follow-up communication. If no partnership materialises, we will delete your data within 12 months of your submission unless you request earlier deletion.
priv_under_the_uk_gdpr: Under the UK GDPR, you have the right to:
priv_access_request_a_copy: Access — Request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
priv_erasure_request_deletion_of: Erasure — Request deletion of your data ("right to be forgotten").
priv_restriction_request_that_we: Restriction — Request that we limit how we use your data.
priv_data_portability_request_your: Data portability — Request your data in a structured, commonly used format.
priv_object_object_to_processing: Object — Object to processing based on legitimate interest.
priv_to_exercise_any_of: To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at enquiries@novaural.com. We will respond within 30 days.
priv_we_take_appropriate_technical: We take appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your data, including:
priv_all_data_submitted_through: All data submitted through our website is transmitted over HTTPS/TLS encryption.
priv_access_to_enquiry_data: Access to enquiry data is restricted to authorised Novaural personnel only.
priv_we_regularly_review_our: We regularly review our security practices.
priv_your_data_may_be: Your data may be processed by Formspree's servers, which may be located outside the United Kingdom. Where this occurs, we ensure that appropriate safeguards are in place in accordance with UK GDPR requirements.
priv_this_website_is_not: This website is not directed at children under the age of 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe we have inadvertently collected such data, please contact us and we will delete it promptly.
priv_if_you_are_unsatisfied: If you are unsatisfied with how we handle your data, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO):
priv_we_may_update_this: We may update this policy from time to time. Any changes will be reflected on this page with an updated "Last updated" date. We encourage you to review this page periodically.
priv_for_any_privacyrelated_questions: For any privacy-related questions or requests, please contact:

hiw_hero_intro: Current best practice excels at measuring what reaches the ear — REM, REAR, audiogram targets. But it has no standardised way to measure what the patient actually perceives and discriminates after fitting. That's the gap ReFittingLab fills — with a patent-backed, evidence-based algorithm that includes 5 independent safety layers and a multi-factor computation engine.
hiw_gap_subtitle: After applying a prescriptive formula and verifying with REM, the clinician enters the fine-tuning phase — and suddenly loses access to objective data.
hiw_gap_callout_body:
Pre-fitting has excellent objective tools: audiogram, REM/REAR, RECD, prescriptive formulas (NAL-NL2, DSLv5.0, manufacturer-based).
Post-fitting fine-tuning has almost none. The clinician adjusts based on subjective patient reports and their own experience — with no objective way to measure whether word understanding actually improved after a gain change. Did the patient's ability to distinguish similar-sounding words get better? Worse? By how much? Nobody knows.

ReFittingLab bridges this gap with a complete fine-tuning verification system: it evaluates the patient's ability to discriminate and understand speech sounds — using minimal pairs, word lists, and speech-in-noise tests — then maps the results directly to per-band gain adjustments. The entire process is backed by a multi-factor computation engine with 5 independent safety layers, producing targeted, bounded recommendations that improve real-world word understanding, not just acoustic output.
hiw_workflow_subtitle: ReFittingLab does not replace your fitting software or prescriptive formula. It enters the workflow after the initial fitting, exactly where objective data disappears.
hiw_highlight_step_text_1: This is where ReFittingLab enters. Using extremely select minimal pairs — curated through rigorous phonological, phonetic, and psychometric criteria — the system evaluates the patient's ability to discriminate speech sounds across frequency bands at multiple intensity levels (50, 65, 80 dB SPL), and outputs specific, per-band gain adjustments (ΔG50, ΔG65, ΔG80) validated through 5 independent safety checks.
hiw_highlight_step_text_2: The clinician then applies these adjustments in their fitting software and re-tests to confirm measurable improvement. No guesswork. No waiting for the next appointment.
hiw_process_subtitle: A complete verification session takes 20–30 minutes and produces actionable, per-band gain adjustment recommendations — each validated against 5 safety layers.
hiw_algorithm_subtitle: The core algorithm translates a perceptual error into a precise, band-specific gain change through a multi-stage pipeline. Each adjustment is bounded, safe, and grounded in the patient's individual audiometric profile.
hiw_algorithm_description: This is not a generic "boost highs if speech is unclear" approach. Each phoneme contrast maps to a specific set of distinctive features (Jakobson–Fant–Halle framework), which in turn correspond to specific frequency bands via the Nearest Frequency Matching (NFM) mechanism — automatically adapting to any hearing device's band configuration (5-band consumer earbud or 20-band clinical aid). The multi-factor formula considers:
hiw_safety_subtitle: Every adjustment recommendation passes through 5 independent safety checks before being presented to the clinician. This isn't just gain boosting — it's a validated, bounded computation with multiple protective mechanisms.
hiw_safety_formula_ucl: Output = 65 dB + G65current + Δ dB → must not exceed UCL − 7 dB
hiw_safety_formula_dr: DR = UCL − HTL → if DR < 30 dB, Δ dB capped at 2 dB
hiw_safety_formula_mpo: Headroom = MPO − (65 + G65) → Δ dB must not exceed headroom
hiw_safety_formula_cr: CR = 30 / (30 + G80 − G50) → if CR > 3:1, alert generated
hiw_safety_constants_note: All safety constants are derived from published clinical guidelines (ASHA, BSA, NAL-NL2, ANSI S3.5-1997, Keidser & Moore) and are configurable per-deployment. The system generates a ValidationReport classifying the overall recommendation as PROCEED, PROCEED WITH CAUTION, or REVIEW REQUIRED.
hiw_compat_subtitle: ReFittingLab is manufacturer-agnostic. It generates per-band gain adjustment recommendations that you apply in whichever fitting software you already use. The NFM mechanism automatically adapts to any device's band configuration — from basic hearing aids with 4 bands to premium devices with 20 or more.
hiw_trademark_disclaimer: All product names mentioned are trademarks of their respective owners. Novaural is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by any hearing aid manufacturer. ReFittingLab is an independent verification system.
hiw_equipment_subtitle: ReFittingLab requires no booth modifications and no proprietary hardware. The entire setup fits on your existing clinic desk — a laptop, a speaker, and a sound level meter. That's it.
hiw_speaker_description: A powered near-field monitor with flat frequency response across the speech range (200 Hz – 8 kHz). Studio reference monitors designed for accurate audio reproduction are ideal — widely available and affordable.
hiw_slm_description: An IEC 61672-1 Class 2 compliant sound level meter. Used only during the initial calibration to verify SPL at the patient position. Once calibrated, it stays in the drawer until you change rooms or equipment.
hiw_software_description: Runs on any Windows or Mac laptop. Includes a built-in calibration wizard, language packs, and the full multi-factor algorithm with all 5 safety layers. No proprietary dongles, no per-session limits.
hiw_calibration_intro: The software includes a guided calibration wizard that takes under 5 minutes:
hiw_calibration_note: The process follows IEC 60645-1 calibration principles. No specialist technician required — any audiologist can complete it independently.
hiw_cost_description: Less than the cost of a single hearing aid return. Equipment lasts thousands of sessions with no consumables. If you already own a suitable speaker and sound level meter, your cost is zero.
hiw_advisory_note: Not sure which hardware to choose? We're happy to advise on the minimum specification that meets clinical requirements. We are not affiliated with, sponsored by, or receiving commissions from any hardware manufacturer — we only recommend what is sufficient for reliable, standards-compliant sessions.
hiw_outcomes_subtitle: ReFittingLab creates measurable value across the entire hearing care ecosystem — from NHS trusts managing thousands of patients to private practices competing on quality.
hiw_cta_subtitle: We're seeking clinical partners to validate ReFittingLab in real-world settings. If you're an audiologist, hearing aid dispenser, or clinic manager, we'd love to hear from you.
explore_hiw_desc: The full 8-step clinical workflow, the NFM algorithm, 5 safety layers, calibration, and equipment — everything an audiologist needs to evaluate the platform.
explore_consumer_desc: How the same phonemic discrimination technology applies to consumer hearables, gaming headsets, AR/VR audio, and over-the-counter hearing devices.
